same local Ollama model as baseline
same local Ollama model as baseline
same local Ollama model as baseline
arms.hypothesis: Executable domain micro contracts can move the local model past the repeated domain_behavior_failed plateau without a deterministic domain kernel.

arms.modelPolicy: same local Ollama model as baseline
arms.hypothesis: Public domain API contracts, export-surface normalization, and checkpoint scripts can prevent local-model export collapse while preserving hidden verifier isolation.

arms.modelPolicy: deterministic scaffold and deterministic domain kernel; no model-authored product code
arms.hypothesis: A known-good domain kernel plus scaffold should pass the public and hidden FSCRUD verifier, proving R30's remaining blocker was executable domain behavior.

arms.modelPolicy: same local Ollama model as baseline after deterministic domain kernel setup
arms.hypothesis: With the domain kernel protected, local PL can complete server, UI, docs, manifest, and verification-report work without corrupting protected artifacts.
arms.result: Observed in live-fscrud-r31-domain-kernel-20260504-1247: 93/100 flow_failed with domain behavior passing and ui_surface_incomplete remaining.

arms.modelPolicy: same local Ollama model as R31 after deterministic domain kernel setup
arms.hypothesis: With the domain kernel protected and explicit nearby UI entity/action gates, local PL can close the remaining ui_surface_incomplete failure without frontier edits.
arms.result: Observed in live-fscrud-r32-ui-surface-20260504-1448: 80/100 flow_failed with domain behavior passing and ui_surface_incomplete remaining.

arms.modelPolicy: same local Ollama model as R32 after deterministic domain kernel and deterministic UI skeleton setup
arms.hypothesis: With domain behavior and UI surface coverage supplied deterministically and protected, local PL can complete the remaining server, docs, manifest, report, and final wiring work without corrupting protected artifacts.
arms.claimBoundary: This is a deterministic UI skeleton control. A pass would not prove the local model can generate the UI surface; it would only support the narrower claim that local inference can perform constrained integration around deterministic behavioral and presentation contracts.
arms.result: Observed in live-fscrud-r33-ui-skeleton-reviewgate-20260504-1624 after fixing invalid review syntax: verifier passed 87/100 with hiddenOraclePassed=true, domainBehaviorPassed=true, uiSurface=true, and no hard failures, but strict public review failed after 4 rounds because the local model did not create run-manifest.json or verification-report.md.

arms.modelPolicy: same local Ollama model as R33 after deterministic domain kernel, deterministic UI skeleton, and deterministic handoff artifacts setup
arms.hypothesis: If README, manifest, and verification report are supplied deterministically and protected, the local model can complete only src/server.js integration around deterministic domain and UI contracts.
arms.claimBoundary: This is a server-only local integration diagnostic. A pass would not prove local generation of UI, docs, or manifest artifacts; it would isolate whether local inference can complete the remaining server integration task.
arms.result: Observed in live-fscrud-r34-server-only-rerun-20260504-1710 after adding an R34-specific public gate: 100/100 verified_pass with publicGatePassed=true, hiddenOraclePassed=true, and domainBehaviorPassed=true. Strict-root rerun live-fscrud-r34-server-only-strict-root-20260504-1727 scored 100/100 on content and passed public/domain behavior checks, but failed hidden verification with path_root_isolation_failed because the model created a nested fscrud-01/src/server.js. Path-guard rerun live-fscrud-r34-server-only-path-guard-20260504-1801 passed 100/100 with publicGatePassed=true, hiddenOraclePassed=true, domainBehaviorPassed=true, and pathRootIsolation=true after runner/public-gate path discipline hardening. Repeated same-commit batch live-fscrud-r34-server-only-path-guard-k3-20260504-1820 passed R34 3/3 at 100/100 with pathRootIsolation=true, no leaks, no nested roots, executable domain behavior passing, and npm tests exiting 0. Next step is broader artifact responsibility, not another server-only repetition.

arms.modelPolicy: same local Ollama model as R34 after deterministic domain kernel, deterministic UI skeleton, and deterministic server integration setup
arms.hypothesis: With product behavior supplied deterministically and protected, local PL can make the local model generate README, run-manifest.json, and verification-report.md under strict review.
arms.claimBoundary: This is a handoff-artifacts diagnostic. A pass would not prove local generation of domain, UI, or server code; it would isolate whether local inference can generate compliant handoff artifacts around deterministic product behavior.
arms.result: First smoke live-fscrud-r35-handoff-artifacts-20260504-1925 scored 91/100 with hiddenOraclePassed=true, domainBehaviorPassed=true, and pathRootIsolation=true, but flow_failed because verification-report.md was missing and the repair response made no observable workspace progress. Split-card follow-up live-fscrud-r35-handoff-artifacts-split-20260504-1945 scored 87/100 with hiddenOraclePassed=true, domainBehaviorPassed=true, uiSurface=true, and pathRootIsolation=true, but created only README.md. Exact-template follow-up live-fscrud-r35-handoff-artifacts-template-20260504-1955 again scored 87/100 with protected product checks green, but the local model wrote a generic README and ignored readme_missing:npm test before reaching run-manifest.json or verification-report.md. R35 now isolates a local artifact-following and repair-compliance blocker rather than a product behavior blocker.

arms.modelPolicy: same local Ollama model as R35 after deterministic domain kernel, deterministic UI skeleton, and deterministic server integration setup
arms.hypothesis: If freeform handoff artifact generation is the R35 blocker, the local model can instead create one compliant structured handoff-source.json that deterministic tooling renders into README, run-manifest.json, and verification-report.md.
arms.claimBoundary: This is a structured-source diagnostic. A pass would not prove local generation of domain, UI, server, or final rendered handoff files; it would isolate whether local inference can generate a compliant structured source for deterministic handoff rendering.
arms.result: Observed in live-fscrud-r36-structured-handoff-20260504-2008 and live-fscrud-r36-structured-handoff-template-20260504-2025: both scored 82/100 with hiddenOraclePassed=true, domainBehaviorPassed=true, uiSurface=true, seed integrity passing, and pathRootIsolation=true, but flow_failed at the structured source review. The first run wrote {}, and the exact-template follow-up wrote an invented short object instead of the supplied JSON. R36 does not support the hypothesis that a single structured source file is enough under the current natural-language local runner.
